gpt55_review_summary: GPT-5.5 found that run-status sidecars could be paired with unrelated successful runs and could smuggle raw objects through allowed fields, including wrapper action. I required non-empty matching task keys, strict submit-payload provenance, and allowlisted type-checked schemas for wrappers/status/tasks/submit-payloads; final re-review returned no findings.
verification: poetry run pytest -q tests/test_release_bundle.py tests/test_databricks_runs.py tests/test_public_package.py tests/test_databricks_job.py tests/test_project_governance.py (64 passed)
GPT-5.5 review found unrelated-run and nested raw-field bypasses plus wrapper-action smuggling; all were fixed and final re-review returned no findings
what_changed: Added sanitized Databricks runs/submit payload provenance to compact run-status summaries, including canonical payload SHA-256, task keys, cluster node types, single-node status, data-security modes, and AWS g5 detection.
Required release-bundle Databricks run-status sidecars to include successful terminal run status plus strict single-node AWS g5 SINGLE_USER submit-payload proof.
Hardened run-status sidecar validation with strict wrapper/status/task/submit-payload schemas so raw Jobs API responses or nested sensitive fields cannot be bundled under allowed field names.
why: The V1 release bundle must prove Databricks artifacts came from target AWS g5/UC runs, not merely from an unrelated successful Jobs run or a sidecar containing raw API payloads.
